underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Oklahoma U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). OPEN seat — Mullin resigned to become DHS Secretary; appointed Sen. Armstrong cannot seek term.
method: Anchor: 2020 Inhofe +21 (53.4-42.5). 2022 Lankford +35.8 (full-term). 2022 special Mullin +26.6. 2024 Trump won OK by ~33pts. Now OPEN seat — Mullin resigned March 2026 (DHS); Trump-aligned Kevin Hern emerged as leading R. D bench is weak; OK Dem hasn't carried any county in recent Senate races. Modeled R margin ~ N(30, 8).
notes: Open seat after Mullin → DHS Secretary (March 2026). Hern (R-OK leading candidate, Trump-endorsed) likely nominee. Recent OK Senate margins: Inhofe+21 (2020), Mullin+26.6 (2022 special), Lankford+35.8 (2022 full). Mean ~28; trending up with Trump's deepening hold. State PVI Trump+33 in 2024. Model μ=30 — between Mullin's special (26.6) and Lankford's full (35.8); open-seat slightly compresses margin. Kalshi YES asks: P25 0.68, P29 0.54, P31 0.47, P33 0.41 — market median ~30, aligned.
